data.title: Cryptocurrency market wipes out $130 billion in 24 hours
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market faced heavy selling pressure in recent hours as traders reacted to large whale movements and rising outflows from spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs). As of press time, the global crypto market capitalization stood at $3.88 trillion, down from $4.01 trillion just 24 hours earlier, a $130 billion loss
source: https://finbold.com/cryptocurrency-market-wipes-out-130-billion-in-24-hours/

data.title: Public Companies Quietly Loading Up on Solana, Turning Treasuries Into Yield Machines
data.description:
Many publicly traded companies are discreetly acquiring large amounts of Solana, a leading cryptocurrency, as part of their investment strategies. This strategic move is enabling them to transform their treasury reserves into powerful sources of yield, allowing them to capitalize on the growing potential of digital assets while maximizing their financial returns.
source: https://blocknews.com/public-companies-quietly-loading-up-on-solana-turning-treasuries-into-yield-machines/

data.title: Urgent iOS Update Released After Zero-Click Hack Targets Crypto
data.description:
Summary: Why is the Apple zero-click vulnerability dangerous for crypto users? The flaw allows hackers to access devices and potentially steal wallet apps or exchange credentials without user action. This makes crypto holdings especially vulnerable because transactions are irreversible. Users are urged to update devices, use strong passwords, and consider extra security like hardware wallets or multi-factor authentication. Apple has issued an emergency update to fix a zero-click vulnerability in iPhones, iPads, and Macs that could allow hackers to access sensitive data, including crypto wallets
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/08/25/urgent-ios-update-released-after-zero-click-hack-targets-crypto/

data.title: Tokenized Real-World Assets May Unlock $400T TradFi Market, Says Animoca
data.description:

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/tokenized-real-world-assets-may-unlock-400t-tradfi-market/

data.title: Ethereum's Recent Outperformance Over Bitcoin May Be Short-Lived
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Wang Feng, the founder of Blueport Interactive, recently tweeted that the recent trend of Ethereum outperforming Bitcoin is likely due to differences in narrative timing, and this window may be brief, leaving investors with little time to adjust their positions.

In the medium to long term, both cryptocurrencies share the same pool of macro funds, regulatory benefits, and market capitalization trends. Ethereum should be viewed as a beta amplifier for Bitcoin rather than a substitute.

Historical patterns indicate that during the 2017 ICO and 2020 DeFi cycles, Ethereum's popularity actually enhanced Bitcoin's safe-haven premium. Ethereum acts as leverage, while Bitcoin serves as an anchor, with large funds eventually returning to Bitcoin to realize profits after successful ventures.
